content: Kicked off senior designer search with Jenny and Priya. Role scope: product + brand, 1 level above Jenny, backfill headcount approved in Feb budget. Target: 12-week hire, $160–180k base depending on equity negotiation.
summary: Senior designer search initiated with budget approved

content: Senior designer hire fills critical gap: Jenny at capacity on product work, brand asset debt mounting (Figma audit showed 200+ unused components). Hire enables parallel tracks on design system and customer-facing marketing materials.
summary: Designer hire justification: capacity + brand debt

content: Priya sent candidate slate: 8 initial prospects. Screened 6 this week. Notes: 2 overqualified (ex-director roles, looking part-time), 1 early-stage burnout signal, 3 solid. Moving 3 to Jenny interview.
Jenny's feedback on first round: loved candidate #2 (ex-Linear, 8yr exp, strong systems thinking), middling on #1 (good craft, no B2B), passed on #3 (overengineered portfolio). Pushing #2 to onsite.

Onsite scheduled for Yuki: 4/14, morning design critique + lunch with Jenny, afternoon Sebastien + me. Priya prepping candidate brief (Clude stack: Figma, Notion, Linear, Slack; design process doc attached).
Sebastien asked about Yuki's comp expectations in 1:1. Priya intel: last package at Linear was $175k + 0.08% equity. We're at $165k offer ceiling for this cycle. Flag for negotiation.
summary: Comp gap identified before onsite: $175k vs $165k ceiling

content: Designer onsite workflow: (1) 90min portfolio deep-dive with Jenny before candidate arrives, (2) live design exercise (2hr, open tools), (3) values/process Q&A, (4) team lunch, (5) exec chat. Feedback form due EOD same day.
summary: Documented senior designer onsite interview process

content: Yuki onsite happened today. Jenny: 'Design thinking is sharp, asked good questions about our data model. Worried about pace—wants to move slow on decision-making.' Sebastien positive on culture fit. I'm neutral pending full debrief.
summary: Yuki onsite: strong design, decision-speed concern flagged

content: Debrief meeting: Jenny +4 (Ari, Sebastien, Anya, me). Consensus: hire her. Ari raised velocity questions but Jenny said 'speed comes with codebase intimacy.' Sebastien: 'Let's move to offer.' Priya to prepare.
summary: Hire decision made on Yuki, proceeding to offer

content: Offer drafted: $165k base, 0.06% equity, 4-week onboarding plan (design systems audit weeks 1–2, sprint integration weeks 3–4). Start date: 5/6/26. Priya calling Yuki tomorrow morning.

Priya texted: Yuki counter on equity—asking 0.10%. Wants time to discuss with advisor. Not a hard no. Gave 48-hr window. Anya says 0.08% is max we can stretch without board approval. Waiting on response.
I notice I'm less patient with drawn-out negotiations than Sebastien is. Want closure; he sees it as relationship-building. Probably a liability on exec hires. Made note to discuss with Anya.
Yuki accepted at 0.08% equity split the difference. Signed offer letter came back 4/21. James (new backend hire, started Mar) was on async Slack agreeing design hire solves bottleneck he'd flagged.
summary: Yuki accepted offer at 0.08% equity, signed

content: Sent Yuki welcome packet: company handbook (Linear doc), Figma org access, design rituals calendar (design review Tues/Thurs), onboarding checklist. Intro call scheduled 4/28 to walk through first-week priorities.
summary: Onboarding materials sent, intro call scheduled 4/28

content: Lessons learned from Yuki hire: (1) Ex-big-company designers negotiate equity hard; budget 0.08–0.10% for senior hires. (2) Speed concerns from eng lead worth taking seriously—onboarding plan should address. (3) Async offer feedback form cuts debrief time 30%.
summary: Designer hire lessons: equity expectations, eng concerns, async docs

content: Drafted hiring recap memo for Sebastien: Yuki starts 5/6, design systems audit first sprint, Jenny mentors onboarding. No surprises. Flagged next gap: content/web design (copywriting, landing pages). Priya to scope in May.
summary: Hiring recap sent; flagged next design hire for pipeline
